theme_notes: Lines draw from Trump's naval blockade of Iran sending Brent crude past $118 (up 6%), King Charles III's historic speech to Congress urging NATO/Ukraine unity, PSG's 5-4 Champions League classic over Bayern, and the Fed holding rates with a record 4 dissents as gas hits $4.23/gallon.
settled: PSG 5, Bayern 4. You two: eerily settled by comparison.
The Fed held rates. You held your own. Barely, but still.
King Charles addressed Congress. Your balance addressed itself. Somehow.
youOwe: Less than a gallon of gas at $4.23. Almost two, if you're an optimist.
The Fed logged 4 dissents today. Your wallet logged zero excuses.
King Charles crossed the Atlantic. You can't cross a Venmo threshold.
Oil's up 6% today. Your excuses are up more.
About 12 gallons of premium in Trump's America. Not impressive.
PSG's third-string winger earns more per minute than this is worth.
Brent crude hit $118 today. Your financial restraint hit zero.
Trump blockaded Iran. Your partner is quietly blockading respect.
Powell would call this 'a matter of concern.' So does your partner.
Gas is $4.23 a gallon and you're burning through goodwill faster.
The Iran war costs $25B over two months. You're a rounding error — but a loud one.
King Charles urged NATO to stay unified. Nobody here is unified.
Kimmel kept his show despite the FCC. You won't keep this relationship at this rate.
Trump threatened Iran with an extended blockade. Yours is getting extended too.
At $118 a barrel, at least oil has geopolitics as an excuse.
The Iran blockade will end before this gets paid off.
Brent crude is volatile and this debt is less stable. Both at records.
The Pentagon spent $25B in two months on the Iran war. You spent this. Differently bad.
theyOwe: They owe you less than a gallon of gas. The indignity costs more.
Not enough to matter. Enough to remember forever.
Even the lone Fed dissenter wouldn't burn political capital on this.
About 12 gallons of gas at AAA prices. Start the meter.
Kimmel kept his show under pressure. Keep this invoice the same way.
The Fed raised nothing. You are raising this. Good instincts.
Oil jumped 6% in a day. Your patience has not budged.
King Charles made a better case to Congress than they've made to you.
Not enough to leave. Enough to mention at every dinner for years.
The Fed held rates steady. You should not.
Brent crude is at $118. Their goodwill trades lower.
Trump's Iran blockade costs less per day than this outstanding balance.
King Charles addressed Congress with more dignity than their last payment message.
The FCC is reviewing ABC licenses. Review your exit options.
Powell ends his term with 4 dissents. End this arrangement with one ultimatum.
Iran is under a naval blockade. So is your generosity.
This is enough to fund your own Iran policy. Consider sanctions.
King Charles urged NATO unity. Nobody here is paying dues either.
